title: Application of Attention Mechanism with Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM) and CNN for Human Conflict Detection using Computer Vision
authors: Not specified in the provided text
publication_venue: Not specified in the provided text
abstract: This paper explores the application of attention mechanisms combined with BiLSTM and CNN for human conflict detection using computer vision. It addresses the limitations of conventional surveillance monitoring systems and proposes a deep learning approach for automated incident detection in surveillance videos.

methodology.application: Human conflict detection in surveillance videos using computer vision

challenges: Low quality of video frames in public datasets
Variations in video resolution, frame frequency, lighting conditions, and camera angles

key_findings: Increasing dataset size from 500 to 1,600 videos improves model accuracy by 6%
Large datasets and transfer learning are important for effective surveillance systems

references.contribution: Evaluated deep learning techniques for violence detection in videos
Analyzed trajectory of movements and orientation of body limbs to detect violent behavior
Introduced hierarchical hidden Markov model (HHMM) for recognizing aggressive attitudes
Combined probabilistic PCA with Markov Random Fields for violence detection
Proposed technique evaluating elements like blood, flames, movement intensity, and sound volume
